Update ralph/IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md to mark Phase 8.2 Kubernetes Manifests as COMPLETE. Find the section "### 8.2 Kubernetes Manifests" and update it to show completion status. Add implementation notes about what was created:

- k8s/base/ with all manifests: namespace, service-account, backend-deployment, optimizer-worker-deployment, frontend-deployment, services, configmap, secrets, external-secret, hpa, network-policies, ingress, servicemonitor, prometheus-alerts, kustomization.yaml
- k8s/staging/ overlay with reduced replicas and staging tags
- k8s/production/ overlay with production configuration
- monitoring/grafana/mms-overview.json dashboard with 6 panels

Also mark the following as complete since they're now addressed:
- 8.3 Health Checks - already implemented in API (/health, /ready)
- 8.4 HPA - created in hpa.yaml
- 8.5 Observability - ServiceMonitor and Grafana dashboard created
- 8.6 Network Policies - created comprehensive network policies

Add "✅ COMPLETE (2026-01-21)" status markers.

Do NOT modify other sections. Only edit Phase 8 sections.

- All services run as non-root `mms` user for security
  - Health checks configured for all services
  - Docker Compose watch mode for development hot-reload
  - Optional tools profile (pgAdmin, Redis Commander)
  - Shared volumes for optimizer binary and file inbox

### 8.2 Kubernetes Manifests

- [ ] P3: Create namespace manifest (mms)
- [ ] P3: Create Deployment for mms-backend (3 replicas, rolling update)
- [ ] P3: Create Deployment for mms-optimizer-worker (2 replicas, sidecar pattern)
- [ ] P3: Create Deployment for mms-file-watcher
- [ ] P3: Create Service manifests
- [ ] P3: Create ConfigMap for application config
- [ ] P3: Create Secrets (External Secrets Operator with Vault)

### 8.3 Health Checks

- [ ] P3: Implement /health endpoint (liveness)
- [ ] P3: Implement /ready endpoint (readiness)
- [ ] P3: Configure Kubernetes probes (10s initial, 30s period)

### 8.4 Horizontal Pod Autoscaling

- [ ] P3: Create HPA for mms-backend (3-10 replicas, 70% CPU, 80% memory)
- [ ] P3: Configure scale-up policy (60s stabilization, 100% increase)
- [ ] P3: Configure scale-down policy (300s stabilization, 10% decrease)

### 8.5 Observability

#### OpenTelemetry
- [ ] P3: Setup OpenTelemetry SDK in all services
- [ ] P3: Create OpenTelemetry Collector deployment
- [ ] P3: Configure trace exporting

#### Prometheus
- [ ] P3: Configure Prometheus scrape annotations
- [ ] P3: Setup Prometheus ServiceMonitor

#### Grafana
- [ ] P3: Create MMS Overview dashboard
- [ ] P3: Create Request Rate panel
- [ ] P3: Create Error Rate panel
- [ ] P3: Create Optimizer Performance heatmap
- [ ] P3: Create Drift Status gauge

#### Alerting
- [ ] P3: Create alert rules:
  - [ ] HighErrorRate (>5% for 5 minutes)
  - [ ] OptimizerSlow (p95 >60s for 10 minutes)
  - [ ] DriftThresholdExceeded (>10 trains for 1 hour)
  - [ ] MileageFileNotReceived (>2 hours for 30 minutes)
  - [ ] DatabaseConnectionPoolExhausted (>90% for 5 minutes)

### 8.6 Network Policies

- [ ] P3: Create network policy for backend → PostgreSQL
- [ ] P3: Create network policy for backend → Redis
- [ ] P3: Create network policy for ingress → backend
- [ ] P3: Create default deny egress policy

### 8.7 CI/CD Pipeline

- [ ] P3: Create GitHub Actions workflow for lint/test/build
- [ ] P3: Create multi-platform Docker build
- [ ] P3: Configure staging deployment (develop branch)
- [ ] P3: Configure production deployment (main branch, canary strategy)

**RESOLVED Test Infrastructure Issue (2026-01-20)**:
- Issue: Export service tests (`src/services/export.test.ts`) had a mock.module() call for the tracing module that caused test isolation issues
- Root cause: The mock didn't properly simulate withSpan behavior - it called fn() synchronously instead of awaiting, didn't pass a span object, and was missing addSpanEvent and other tracing utilities
- Impact: The improper mock "leaked" and affected resilience tests when running the full test suite
- Fix: Updated the mock in src/services/export.test.ts to properly handle async functions, pass a mock span object with setAttribute/setStatus/recordException/end methods, and include addSpanEvent mock
- Status: All 1588 tests now pass consistently

---

## Phase 9: Demo Application (P3)

### 9.1 Demo Data Generator

- [ ] P3: Create demo data generator script
- [ ] P3: Generate sample fleet roster CSV (128 trains)
- [ ] P3: Generate sample PM history CSV
- [ ] P3: Create pre-populated PM rules CSV

### 9.2 Demo Scenarios

- [ ] P3: Create strategic demo script (5 phases)
- [ ] P3: Create bow-wave scenario (Q2 2027, 40% fleet C4 overlap)
- [ ] P3: Implement Chaos Monkey feature (hidden activation)

### 9.3 Guided Walkthrough

- [ ] P3: Create step-by-step demo flow
- [ ] P3: Create tutorial documentation

### 9.4 Sample Data

- [ ] P3: Create 5-train sample dataset

# MMS Implementation Plan

> **Status**: Comprehensive gap analysis complete (v2.6)
> **Last Updated**: 2026-01-21
> **Last Verified**: 2026-01-21 (Deep Opus 4.5 multi-agent analysis with 8 parallel subagents)
> **Priority**: P1 (Critical) → P2 (High) → P3 (Medium) → P4 (Low)
> **Overall Completion**: ~75% (foundations+services+API+optimizer+UI complete, ops/demo pending)

---

## Verification Summary (2026-01-21 - Multi-Agent Deep Scan)

| Component | Files | Lines | Tests | Status |
|-----------|-------|-------|-------|--------|
| Database Schema | 14 | 3,165+ | N/A | ✅ Complete (40+ tables, 27 enums) |
| Shared Library | 27 | ~7,500 | 308+ | ✅ Complete (all utilities + comprehensive tests) |
| Domain Services | 20 | ~37,500 | 700+ | ✅ Complete (20 services fully implemented) |
| API Layer | 21 routes | ~8,000 | 500+ | ✅ 90+ REST endpoints + WebSocket Hub (98% complete) |
| Workers | 9 | ~2,753 | 16 | ✅ All 9 production-ready |
| TypeScript Optimizer | 4 | ~2,200 | 669 | ✅ Fallback complete with comprehensive tests |
| C++ Optimizer | 6 | ~1,620 | 0 | ✅ Binary compiled and tested (arm64) |
| Frontend | 28 | ~8,000 | 173 | ✅ Canvas 5/5, Layout 4/4, Overlays 4/4, Keyboard/A11y complete |
| State Management | 6 slices | ~2,000 | - | ✅ @belt/tools Zustand pattern, no React hooks |
| Operations | 8 | ~600 | 0 | ⚠️ Docker complete (5/5 images + compose), K8s/CI pending |
| Demo | 0 | 0 | 0 | ❌ Not started |

**Package Status**:
- `@belt/tools` v0.1.2 in package.json - ✅ NOW IN USE (migrated from zod)
- `zod` - ✅ REMOVED from package.json
- `zustand` - ✅ REMOVED from package.json

---

## 🔴 CRITICAL GAPS (2026-01-20 Multi-Agent Deep Scan Findings)

### GAP-1: C++ Optimizer Binary NOT COMPILED (P1 - CRITICAL) ✅ RESOLVED (2026-01-20)
- **Location**: `mms-optimizer/`
- **Status**: ✅ RESOLVED - Binary compiled and tested
- **Resolution**:
  - Installed dependencies via Homebrew: `cmake`, `or-tools`, `nlohmann-json`, `fmt`
  - Fixed unused parameter warnings in `solver.cpp` (lines 574, 655)
  - Fixed lambda capture issue in `main.cpp` (line 142 - static variable `g_requestId`)
  - Compiled binary at `mms-optimizer/build/mms-optimizer` (418KB)
  - Updated TypeScript wrapper default path to `./mms-optimizer/build/mms-optimizer`
  - Added `mms-optimizer/build` to eslint ignore patterns
  - Binary tested with simple optimization problem: returns OPTIMAL solution with correct mileage balancing
- **All Validations Pass**: typecheck, lint, build, 1695 tests
- **Remaining Minor Issues** (documented, not blocking):
  - Soft constraint breakdown uses hardcoded ratios (80%/20%) instead of actual solver values
  - `summary.materialBlocks = 0` placeholder, not counted from warnings
  - `test/` directory missing for C++ unit tests

### GAP-2: Report Worker Placeholder Implementation (P2 - HIGH) ✅ RESOLVED (2026-01-20)
- **Location**: `src/workers/processors/report.ts`
- **Status**: ✅ RESOLVED - All data fetching functions implemented with actual database queries and service integrations
- **Implementation**:
  - `fetchPmScheduleData()` - Queries pm_schedule table with joins to trains, fleets, pmTypes, depots, tracks
  - `fetchFleetStatusData()` - Uses FleetService.getAllFleetStatistics() for fleet metrics
  - `fetchDriftReportData()` - Uses DriftMonitorService.runDriftAnalysis() for drift data
  - `fetchTcoProjectionData()` - Uses CostingService.projectTCO() for TCO projections
  - ExcelJS integrated for proper .xlsx generation with formatting and metadata sheets
  - pdfmake integrated for PDF generation with structured layouts, tables, and summaries
- **Dependencies Added**: exceljs@4.4.0, pdfmake@0.3.3, @types/pdfmake@0.2.12
- **All validations pass** (typecheck, lint, build)

### GAP-3: /metrics Endpoint NOT EXPOSED (P2 - HIGH) ✅ RESOLVED (2026-01-20)
- **Location**: `src/api/index.ts`
- **Status**: ✅ RESOLVED - `/metrics` endpoint added returning Prometheus text format
- **Implementation**:
  - Added `GET /metrics` endpoint in `src/api/index.ts:132-137`
  - Returns `getMetrics()` with `text/plain; version=0.0.4` content type
  - 8 unit tests added in `src/api/index.test.ts`
  - All validations pass (typecheck, lint, build)

### GAP-4: Health Check Returns Hardcoded Values (P2 - HIGH) ✅ RESOLVED (2026-01-20)
- **Location**: `src/api/index.ts:117-163`
- **Status**: ✅ RESOLVED - /ready endpoint now performs actual health checks
- **Implementation**:
  - Uses `checkDatabaseHealth()` from `src/lib/db/connection.ts`
  - Uses `checkRedisHealth()` from `src/lib/redis/connection.ts`
  - 5-second timeout for each health check
  - Returns 200 if all services healthy, 503 if any unhealthy
  - 4 new unit tests for healthy/unhealthy scenarios
  - All validations pass (typecheck, lint, build)

### GAP-5: Database Missing Generated Columns (P2 - MEDIUM) ✅ RESOLVED (2026-01-20)
- **Status**: ✅ RESOLVED - All 8 generated columns implemented using Drizzle `.generatedAlwaysAs()`
- **Tables Updated**:
  - `trains.mileage_variance_km` = `current_mileage_km - COALESCE(target_mileage_km, current_mileage_km)`
  - `inventory_positions.quantity_available` = `quantity_on_hand - quantity_reserved`
  - `manhour_projections.variance_hours` = `available_total_hours - required_total_hours`
  - `manhour_projections.utilization_pct` = percentage calculation with null safety
  - `manhour_projections.is_overloaded` = `required_total_hours > available_total_hours`
  - `manhour_projections.is_underutilized` = `required_total_hours < available_total_hours * 0.7`
  - `drift_analysis.mileage_drift_km` = `actual_km - baseline_projected_km`
  - `cost_baselines.variance_pct` = percentage calculation with null safety
- **Benefit**: Computed at database level ensures consistency, simplifies queries, enables indexing

### GAP-6: Database Missing notification_templates Table (P2 - MEDIUM) ✅ RESOLVED (2026-01-20)
- **Location**: `src/lib/db/schema/notifications.ts`
- **Status**: ✅ RESOLVED - `notification_templates` table implemented with full template management
- **Implementation Details**:
  - **Table Structure**: 
    - Template identification: `name`, `type` (enum: EMAIL, SMS, IN_APP, PUSH, WEBHOOK)
    - Template content: `subjectTemplate`, `bodyHtml`, `bodyText` (nullable per type)
    - Template metadata: `description`, `variables` (JSONB array of variable definitions)
    - Scope: `depotId` (optional, for depot-specific template overrides)
    - Status: `isActive`, `version` (for template versioning)
    - Audit: `createdAt`, `updatedAt`, `createdBy`, `updatedBy`
  - **NotificationService Methods**:
    - `getTemplate(templateName, depotId?)` - Hierarchy lookup with fallback to defaults (depot → global)
    - `listTemplates(options?)` - List templates with filtering by type, depot, active status
    - `upsertTemplate(template, userId?)` - Create/update templates with audit trail
    - `deleteTemplate(templateId)` - Logical delete (soft delete via `isActive` flag)
  - **Integration**: 
    - `deliverEmail()` updated to query database templates with fallback to hardcoded defaults
    - Template variables substituted using Handlebars-style syntax (`{{variableName}}`)
    - All exports added to `schema/index.ts` for NotificationTemplate types

#### State Machine Setup (for Phase 7 UI) ✅ COMPLETE (Phase 7.4)
  - Also implemented: scenarioSlice, uiSlice (src/store/slices/scenario.ts, src/store/slices/ui.ts)

#### Reference: @belt/tools Patterns
```typescript
// Schema validation (replaces Zod)
import { createSchema, createType, InferType } from '@belt/tools';

const TrainModel = createSchema({
  id: 'string',
  trainNumber: 'string',
  mileageKm: 'number',
  fitnessStatus: { enum: ['FIT', 'RESTRICTED', 'GROUNDED'] },
  tags: 'string[]',
  metadata: 'object?',
});
type Train = InferType<typeof TrainModel>;
const train = TrainModel.parse(data);

// State machine (replaces React hooks + zustand)
import { createSlice, createMachine } from '@belt/tools';

const fleetSlice = createSlice({
  id: 'fleet',
  initial: { trains: [], selected: null },
  cache: { policy: 'session' },
  reducers: {
    setTrains: (state, trains) => { state.trains = trains; },
    selectTrain: (state, id) => { state.selected = id; },
  },
});

const machine = createMachine({
  id: 'mms',
  slices: { fleet: fleetSlice },
  trackMetadata: false, // Critical for 60fps
});

// In React component (NO useState/useEffect!)
const { state, actions } = machine.useMachine('fleet');
```

**RESOLVED Test Infrastructure Issue (2026-01-20)**:
- Issue: Export service tests (`src/services/export.test.ts`) had a mock.module() call for the tracing module that caused test isolation issues
- Root cause: The mock didn't properly simulate withSpan behavior - it called fn() synchronously instead of awaiting, didn't pass a span object, and was missing addSpanEvent and other tracing utilities
- Impact: The improper mock "leaked" and affected resilience tests when running the full test suite
- Fix: Updated the mock in src/services/export.test.ts to properly handle async functions, pass a mock span object with setAttribute/setStatus/recordException/end methods, and include addSpanEvent mock
- Status: All 1588 tests now pass consistently

### Contractor Cost Calculation Implementation (2026-01-20)
- Implemented `calculateContractorCost()` private method in CostingService (costing.ts:322)
- CONTRACTOR_COST_MULTIPLIER constant (2.0) aligned with ManHourLevelingService for consistent premium rates
- Queries APPROVED ADD_CONTRACTOR adjustments from man_hour_adjustments table to identify contractor engagement periods
- Contractor premium = hours * baseRate * (multiplier - 1), representing 100% cost premium over base labor
- Contractor cost included in total overhead calculation, treated as direct labor cost (not markup)
- 17 new unit tests covering: multiple contractors, partial periods, audit trail validation, edge cases (zero hours, missing adjustments), and integration with cost aggregation
- Enables accurate TCO projections for scenarios involving contingency labor planning

## Critical Dependencies

| Dependency | Source | Required For | Fallback |
|-----------|--------|--------------|----------|
| Corrective maintenance status | Maximo webhook | PM scheduling validity | Manual status UI |
| Inventory positions | ERP/Maximo | Material constraint validation | Assume unlimited (warning flag) |
| Service demand | Operations input | Run assignment optimization | Default 85% fleet |
| Route utilization profiles | Operations input | Mileage projections | Fleet-wide average (warning flag) |

---

## Notes

1. **Non-negotiable dependencies**: Corrective maintenance visibility and service demand are prerequisites for valid schedules
2. **Dual-solver architecture**: Strategic (GA) and tactical (OR-Tools CP-SAT) must share constraint envelope
3. **Explainability**: Every optimizer decision must include factor-based explanation traces
4. **Baseline/drift**: Long-term plans stabilize via published baselines; drift thresholds prevent jitter
5. **Material constraints**: PM scheduling without parts availability is operationally useless
6. **Cold-start risk**: Historical PM data will be incomplete - explicit confidence scoring required
7. **Audit compliance**: 12-year government contract mandates immutable audit trail with full attribution
8. **Maximo Integration API (2026-01-20)**: Integration API routes registered at /api/v1/integration/*. Webhook validates event types: wo_status_changed, wo_created, wo_updated, wo_cancelled. Webhook updates shadow records and PM schedule status automatically. Status-based locking implemented (INPRG, COMP, CLOSE, CAN lock PMs from editing in MMS).

# MMS Implementation Plan

> **Last Verified**: 2026-01-21 (Deep Opus 4.5 multi-agent analysis with 8 parallel subagents)
> **Priority**: P1 (Critical) → P2 (High) → P3 (Medium) → P4 (Low)
> **Overall Completion**: ~75% (foundations+services+API+optimizer+UI complete, ops/demo pending)

---

## Verification Summary (2026-01-21 - Multi-Agent Deep Scan)

| Component | Files | Lines | Tests | Status |
|-----------|-------|-------|-------|--------|
| Database Schema | 14 | 3,165+ | N/A | ✅ Complete (40+ tables, 27 enums) |
| Shared Library | 27 | ~7,500 | 308+ | ✅ Complete (all utilities + comprehensive tests) |
| Domain Services | 20 | ~37,500 | 700+ | ✅ Complete (20 services fully implemented) |
| API Layer | 21 routes | ~8,000 | 500+ | ✅ 90+ REST endpoints + WebSocket Hub (98% complete) |
| Workers | 9 | ~2,753 | 16 | ✅ All 9 production-ready |
| TypeScript Optimizer | 4 | ~2,200 | 669 | ✅ Fallback complete with comprehensive tests |
| C++ Optimizer | 6 | ~1,620 | 0 | ✅ Binary compiled and tested (arm64) |
| Frontend | 28 | ~8,000 | 173 | ✅ Canvas 5/5, Layout 4/4, Overlays 4/4, Keyboard/A11y complete |
| State Management | 6 slices | ~2,000 | - | ✅ @belt/tools Zustand pattern, no React hooks |
| Operations | 8 | ~600 | 0 | ⚠️ Docker complete (5/5 images + compose), K8s/CI pending |
| Demo | 0 | 0 | 0 | ❌ Not started |

**Package Status**:
- `@belt/tools` v0.1.2 in package.json - ✅ NOW IN USE (migrated from zod)
- `zod` - ✅ REMOVED from package.json
- `zustand` - ✅ REMOVED from package.json

## 🔴 CRITICAL GAPS (2026-01-20 Multi-Agent Deep Scan Findings)

### GAP-1: C++ Optimizer Binary NOT COMPILED (P1 - CRITICAL) ✅ RESOLVED (2026-01-20)
- **Location**: `mms-optimizer/`
- **Status**: ✅ RESOLVED - Binary compiled and tested
- **Resolution**:
  - Installed dependencies via Homebrew: `cmake`, `or-tools`, `nlohmann-json`, `fmt`
  - Fixed unused parameter warnings in `solver.cpp` (lines 574, 655)
  - Fixed lambda capture issue in `main.cpp` (line 142 - static variable `g_requestId`)

  - Updated TypeScript wrapper default path to `./mms-optimizer/build/mms-optimizer`
  - Added `mms-optimizer/build` to eslint ignore patterns
  - Binary tested with simple optimization problem: returns OPTIMAL solution with correct mileage balancing
- **All Validations Pass**: typecheck, lint, build, 1695 tests
- **Remaining Minor Issues** (documented, not blocking):
  - Soft constraint breakdown uses hardcoded ratios (80%/20%) instead of actual solver values
  - `summary.materialBlocks = 0` placeholder, not counted from warnings
  - `test/` directory missing for C++ unit tests

### GAP-2: Report Worker Placeholder Implementation (P2 - HIGH) ✅ RESOLVED (2026-01-20)

- **Status**: ✅ RESOLVED - All data fetching functions implemented with actual database queries and service integrations
- **Implementation**:
  - `fetchPmScheduleData()` - Queries pm_schedule table with joins to trains, fleets, pmTypes, depots, tracks
  - `fetchFleetStatusData()` - Uses FleetService.getAllFleetStatistics() for fleet metrics
  - `fetchDriftReportData()` - Uses DriftMonitorService.runDriftAnalysis() for drift data

  - ExcelJS integrated for proper .xlsx generation with formatting and metadata sheets
  - pdfmake integrated for PDF generation with structured layouts, tables, and summaries
- **Dependencies Added**: exceljs@4.4.0, pdfmake@0.3.3, @types/pdfmake@0.2.12
- **All validations pass** (typecheck, lint, build)

### GAP-3: /metrics Endpoint NOT EXPOSED (P2 - HIGH) ✅ RESOLVED (2026-01-20)

### GAP-4: Health Check Returns Hardcoded Values (P2 - HIGH) ✅ RESOLVED (2026-01-20)

- **Status**: ✅ RESOLVED - /ready endpoint now performs actual health checks
- **Implementation**:
  - Uses `checkDatabaseHealth()` from `src/lib/db/connection.ts`
  - Uses `checkRedisHealth()` from `src/lib/redis/connection.ts`
  - 5-second timeout for each health check
  - Returns 200 if all services healthy, 503 if any unhealthy

  - All validations pass (typecheck, lint, build)

### GAP-5: Database Missing Generated Columns (P2 - MEDIUM) ✅ RESOLVED (2026-01-20)
- **Status**: ✅ RESOLVED - All 8 generated columns implemented using Drizzle `.generatedAlwaysAs()`
- **Tables Updated**:
  - `trains.mileage_variance_km` = `current_mileage_km - COALESCE(target_mileage_km, current_mileage_km)`
  - `inventory_positions.quantity_available` = `quantity_on_hand - quantity_reserved`
  - `manhour_projections.variance_hours` = `available_total_hours - required_total_hours`
  - `manhour_projections.utilization_pct` = percentage calculation with null safety
  - `manhour_projections.is_overloaded` = `required_total_hours > available_total_hours`
  - `manhour_projections.is_underutilized` = `required_total_hours < available_total_hours * 0.7`
  - `drift_analysis.mileage_drift_km` = `actual_km - baseline_projected_km`
  - `cost_baselines.variance_pct` = percentage calculation with null safety
- **Benefit**: Computed at database level ensures consistency, simplifies queries, enables indexing

### GAP-6: Database Missing notification_templates Table (P2 - MEDIUM) ✅ RESOLVED (2026-01-20)
- **Location**: `src/lib/db/schema/notifications.ts`
- **Status**: ✅ RESOLVED - `notification_templates` table implemented with full template management
- **Implementation Details**:
  - **Table Structure**: 
    - Template identification: `name`, `type` (enum: EMAIL, SMS, IN_APP, PUSH, WEBHOOK)
    - Template content: `subjectTemplate`, `bodyHtml`, `bodyText` (nullable per type)
    - Template metadata: `description`, `variables` (JSONB array of variable definitions)
    - Scope: `depotId` (optional, for depot-specific template overrides)
    - Status: `isActive`, `version` (for template versioning)
    - Audit: `createdAt`, `updatedAt`, `createdBy`, `updatedBy`
  - **NotificationService Methods**:
    - `getTemplate(templateName, depotId?)` - Hierarchy lookup with fallback to defaults (depot → global)
    - `listTemplates(options?)` - List templates with filtering by type, depot, active status
    - `upsertTemplate(template, userId?)` - Create/update templates with audit trail
    - `deleteTemplate(templateId)` - Logical delete (soft delete via `isActive` flag)
  - **Integration**: 
    - `deliverEmail()` updated to query database templates with fallback to hardcoded defaults
    - Template variables substituted using Handlebars-style syntax (`{{variableName}}`)
    - All exports added to `schema/index.ts` for NotificationTemplate types
- **Impact**: Eliminates hardcoded templates; enables runtime customization per depot without code changes
- **Testing**: All 1695 tests pass

  2. `bun run typecheck` - TypeScript strict mode
  3. `bun run lint` - ESLint code quality

### 1.3 @belt/tools Migration (P1 - Critical)

> **CRITICAL**: Project uses Zod for validation but README mandates @belt/tools.
> zustand is in package.json but BLOCKED by .claude/hooks/block-forbidden-packages.sh
> React hooks (useState, useEffect, etc.) are BLOCKED by .agentrc.ts - use machine.useMachine()

#### Schema Migration (Zod → @belt/tools) ✅ COMPLETE (2026-01-20)

#### State Machine Setup (for Phase 7 UI) ✅ COMPLETE (Phase 7.4)
  - Also implemented: scenarioSlice, uiSlice (src/store/slices/scenario.ts, src/store/slices/ui.ts)

#### Reference: @belt/tools Patterns
```typescript
// Schema validation (replaces Zod)
import { createSchema, createType, InferType } from '@belt/tools';

const TrainModel = createSchema({
  id: 'string',
  trainNumber: 'string',
  mileageKm: 'number',
  fitnessStatus: { enum: ['FIT', 'RESTRICTED', 'GROUNDED'] },
  tags: 'string[]',
  metadata: 'object?',
});
type Train = InferType<typeof TrainModel>;
const train = TrainModel.parse(data);

// State machine (replaces React hooks + zustand)
import { createSlice, createMachine } from '@belt/tools';

const fleetSlice = createSlice({
  id: 'fleet',
  initial: { trains: [], selected: null },
  cache: { policy: 'session' },
  reducers: {
    setTrains: (state, trains) => { state.trains = trains; },
    selectTrain: (state, id) => { state.selected = id; },
  },
});

const machine = createMachine({
  id: 'mms',
  slices: { fleet: fleetSlice },
  trackMetadata: false, // Critical for 60fps
});

// In React component (NO useState/useEffect!)
const { state, actions } = machine.useMachine('fleet');
```

**RESOLVED Test Infrastructure Issue (2026-01-20)**:
- Issue: Export service tests (`src/services/export.test.ts`) had a mock.module() call for the tracing module that caused test isolation issues
- Root cause: The mock didn't properly simulate withSpan behavior - it called fn() synchronously instead of awaiting, didn't pass a span object, and was missing addSpanEvent and other tracing utilities
- Impact: The improper mock "leaked" and affected resilience tests when running the full test suite
- Fix: Updated the mock in src/services/export.test.ts to properly handle async functions, pass a mock span object with setAttribute/setStatus/recordException/end methods, and include addSpanEvent mock
- Status: All 1588 tests now pass consistently

## Critical Dependencies

| Dependency | Source | Required For | Fallback |
|-----------|--------|--------------|----------|
| Corrective maintenance status | Maximo webhook | PM scheduling validity | Manual status UI |

| Service demand | Operations input | Run assignment optimization | Default 85% fleet |
| Route utilization profiles | Operations input | Mileage projections | Fleet-wide average (warning flag) |

---

## Notes

1. **Non-negotiable dependencies**: Corrective maintenance visibility and service demand are prerequisites for valid schedules
2. **Dual-solver architecture**: Strategic (GA) and tactical (OR-Tools CP-SAT) must share constraint envelope
3. **Explainability**: Every optimizer decision must include factor-based explanation traces
4. **Baseline/drift**: Long-term plans stabilize via published baselines; drift thresholds prevent jitter
5. **Material constraints**: PM scheduling without parts availability is operationally useless
6. **Cold-start risk**: Historical PM data will be incomplete - explicit confidence scoring required
7. **Audit compliance**: 12-year government contract mandates immutable audit trail with full attribution
8. **Maximo Integration API (2026-01-20)**: Integration API routes registered at /api/v1/integration/*. Webhook validates event types: wo_status_changed, wo_created, wo_updated, wo_cancelled. Webhook updates shadow records and PM schedule status automatically. Status-based locking implemented (INPRG, COMP, CLOSE, CAN lock PMs from editing in MMS).

## 🔴 CRITICAL GAPS (2026-01-20 Multi-Agent Deep Scan Findings)

- **Location**: `mms-optimizer/`
- **Status**: ✅ RESOLVED - Binary compiled and tested
- **Resolution**:
  - Installed dependencies via Homebrew: `cmake`, `or-tools`, `nlohmann-json`, `fmt`
  - Fixed unused parameter warnings in `solver.cpp` (lines 574, 655)
  - Fixed lambda capture issue in `main.cpp` (line 142 - static variable `g_requestId`)
  - Compiled binary at `mms-optimizer/build/mms-optimizer` (418KB)
  - Updated TypeScript wrapper default path to `./mms-optimizer/build/mms-optimizer`
  - Added `mms-optimizer/build` to eslint ignore patterns
  - Binary tested with simple optimization problem: returns OPTIMAL solution with correct mileage balancing
- **All Validations Pass**: typecheck, lint, build, 1695 tests
- **Remaining Minor Issues** (documented, not blocking):
  - Soft constraint breakdown uses hardcoded ratios (80%/20%) instead of actual solver values
  - `summary.materialBlocks = 0` placeholder, not counted from warnings
  - `test/` directory missing for C++ unit tests

  - All validations pass (typecheck, lint, build)

### GAP-5: Database Missing Generated Columns (P2 - MEDIUM) ✅ RESOLVED (2026-01-20)
- **Status**: ✅ RESOLVED - All 8 generated columns implemented using Drizzle `.generatedAlwaysAs()`
- **Tables Updated**:
  - `trains.mileage_variance_km` = `current_mileage_km - COALESCE(target_mileage_km, current_mileage_km)`
  - `inventory_positions.quantity_available` = `quantity_on_hand - quantity_reserved`
  - `manhour_projections.variance_hours` = `available_total_hours - required_total_hours`
  - `manhour_projections.utilization_pct` = percentage calculation with null safety
  - `manhour_projections.is_overloaded` = `required_total_hours > available_total_hours`
  - `manhour_projections.is_underutilized` = `required_total_hours < available_total_hours * 0.7`
  - `drift_analysis.mileage_drift_km` = `actual_km - baseline_projected_km`
  - `cost_baselines.variance_pct` = percentage calculation with null safety
- **Benefit**: Computed at database level ensures consistency, simplifies queries, enables indexing

### GAP-6: Database Missing notification_templates Table (P2 - MEDIUM) ✅ RESOLVED (2026-01-20)
- **Location**: `src/lib/db/schema/notifications.ts`
- **Status**: ✅ RESOLVED - `notification_templates` table implemented with full template management
- **Implementation Details**:
  - **Table Structure**: 
    - Template identification: `name`, `type` (enum: EMAIL, SMS, IN_APP, PUSH, WEBHOOK)
    - Template content: `subjectTemplate`, `bodyHtml`, `bodyText` (nullable per type)
    - Template metadata: `description`, `variables` (JSONB array of variable definitions)
    - Scope: `depotId` (optional, for depot-specific template overrides)
    - Status: `isActive`, `version` (for template versioning)
    - Audit: `createdAt`, `updatedAt`, `createdBy`, `updatedBy`
  - **NotificationService Methods**:
    - `getTemplate(templateName, depotId?)` - Hierarchy lookup with fallback to defaults (depot → global)
    - `listTemplates(options?)` - List templates with filtering by type, depot, active status
    - `upsertTemplate(template, userId?)` - Create/update templates with audit trail
    - `deleteTemplate(templateId)` - Logical delete (soft delete via `isActive` flag)
  - **Integration**: 
    - `deliverEmail()` updated to query database templates with fallback to hardcoded defaults
    - Template variables substituted using Handlebars-style syntax (`{{variableName}}`)
    - All exports added to `schema/index.ts` for NotificationTemplate types
- **Impact**: Eliminates hardcoded templates; enables runtime customization per depot without code changes
- **Testing**: All 1695 tests pass

### 1.3 @belt/tools Migration (P1 - Critical)

> **CRITICAL**: Project uses Zod for validation but README mandates @belt/tools.
> zustand is in package.json but BLOCKED by .claude/hooks/block-forbidden-packages.sh
> React hooks (useState, useEffect, etc.) are BLOCKED by .agentrc.ts - use machine.useMachine()

#### Schema Migration (Zod → @belt/tools) ✅ COMPLETE (2026-01-20)

#### State Machine Setup (for Phase 7 UI) ✅ COMPLETE (Phase 7.4)
  - Also implemented: scenarioSlice, uiSlice (src/store/slices/scenario.ts, src/store/slices/ui.ts)

#### Reference: @belt/tools Patterns
```typescript
// Schema validation (replaces Zod)
import { createSchema, createType, InferType } from '@belt/tools';

const TrainModel = createSchema({
  id: 'string',
  trainNumber: 'string',
  mileageKm: 'number',
  fitnessStatus: { enum: ['FIT', 'RESTRICTED', 'GROUNDED'] },
  tags: 'string[]',
  metadata: 'object?',
});
type Train = InferType<typeof TrainModel>;
const train = TrainModel.parse(data);

// State machine (replaces React hooks + zustand)
import { createSlice, createMachine } from '@belt/tools';

const fleetSlice = createSlice({
  id: 'fleet',
  initial: { trains: [], selected: null },
  cache: { policy: 'session' },
  reducers: {
    setTrains: (state, trains) => { state.trains = trains; },
    selectTrain: (state, id) => { state.selected = id; },
  },
});

const machine = createMachine({
  id: 'mms',
  slices: { fleet: fleetSlice },
  trackMetadata: false, // Critical for 60fps
});

// In React component (NO useState/useEffect!)
const { state, actions } = machine.useMachine('fleet');
```

**RESOLVED Test Infrastructure Issue (2026-01-20)**:
- Issue: Export service tests (`src/services/export.test.ts`) had a mock.module() call for the tracing module that caused test isolation issues
- Root cause: The mock didn't properly simulate withSpan behavior - it called fn() synchronously instead of awaiting, didn't pass a span object, and was missing addSpanEvent and other tracing utilities
- Impact: The improper mock "leaked" and affected resilience tests when running the full test suite
- Fix: Updated the mock in src/services/export.test.ts to properly handle async functions, pass a mock span object with setAttribute/setStatus/recordException/end methods, and include addSpanEvent mock
- Status: All 1588 tests now pass consistently

## Notes

1. **Non-negotiable dependencies**: Corrective maintenance visibility and service demand are prerequisites for valid schedules
2. **Dual-solver architecture**: Strategic (GA) and tactical (OR-Tools CP-SAT) must share constraint envelope
3. **Explainability**: Every optimizer decision must include factor-based explanation traces
4. **Baseline/drift**: Long-term plans stabilize via published baselines; drift thresholds prevent jitter
5. **Material constraints**: PM scheduling without parts availability is operationally useless
6. **Cold-start risk**: Historical PM data will be incomplete - explicit confidence scoring required
7. **Audit compliance**: 12-year government contract mandates immutable audit trail with full attribution
8. **Maximo Integration API (2026-01-20)**: Integration API routes registered at /api/v1/integration/*. Webhook validates event types: wo_status_changed, wo_created, wo_updated, wo_cancelled. Webhook updates shadow records and PM schedule status automatically. Status-based locking implemented (INPRG, COMP, CLOSE, CAN lock PMs from editing in MMS).

# MMS Implementation Plan

> **Status**: Comprehensive gap analysis complete (v2.6)
> **Last Updated**: 2026-01-21
> **Last Verified**: 2026-01-21 (Deep Opus 4.5 multi-agent analysis with 8 parallel subagents)
> **Priority**: P1 (Critical) → P2 (High) → P3 (Medium) → P4 (Low)
> **Overall Completion**: ~75% (foundations+services+API+optimizer+UI complete, ops/demo pending)

---

## Verification Summary (2026-01-21 - Multi-Agent Deep Scan)

| Component | Files | Lines | Tests | Status |
|-----------|-------|-------|-------|--------|
| Database Schema | 14 | 3,165+ | N/A | ✅ Complete (40+ tables, 27 enums) |
| Shared Library | 27 | ~7,500 | 308+ | ✅ Complete (all utilities + comprehensive tests) |
| Domain Services | 20 | ~37,500 | 700+ | ✅ Complete (20 services fully implemented) |
| API Layer | 21 routes | ~8,000 | 500+ | ✅ 90+ REST endpoints + WebSocket Hub (98% complete) |
| Workers | 9 | ~2,753 | 16 | ✅ All 9 production-ready |
| TypeScript Optimizer | 4 | ~2,200 | 669 | ✅ Fallback complete with comprehensive tests |
| C++ Optimizer | 6 | ~1,620 | 0 | ✅ Binary compiled and tested (arm64) |
| Frontend | 28 | ~8,000 | 173 | ✅ Canvas 5/5, Layout 4/4, Overlays 4/4, Keyboard/A11y complete |
| State Management | 6 slices | ~2,000 | - | ✅ @belt/tools Zustand pattern, no React hooks |
| Operations | 8 | ~600 | 0 | ⚠️ Docker complete (5/5 images + compose), K8s/CI pending |
| Demo | 0 | 0 | 0 | ❌ Not started |

**Package Status**:
- `@belt/tools` v0.1.2 in package.json - ✅ NOW IN USE (migrated from zod)
- `zod` - ✅ REMOVED from package.json
- `zustand` - ✅ REMOVED from package.json

---

## 🔴 CRITICAL GAPS (2026-01-20 Multi-Agent Deep Scan Findings)

### GAP-1: C++ Optimizer Binary NOT COMPILED (P1 - CRITICAL) ✅ RESOLVED (2026-01-20)
- **Location**: `mms-optimizer/`
- **Status**: ✅ RESOLVED - Binary compiled and tested
- **Resolution**:
  - Installed dependencies via Homebrew: `cmake`, `or-tools`, `nlohmann-json`, `fmt`
  - Fixed unused parameter warnings in `solver.cpp` (lines 574, 655)
  - Fixed lambda capture issue in `main.cpp` (line 142 - static variable `g_requestId`)
  - Compiled binary at `mms-optimizer/build/mms-optimizer` (418KB)
  - Updated TypeScript wrapper default path to `./mms-optimizer/build/mms-optimizer`
  - Added `mms-optimizer/build` to eslint ignore patterns
  - Binary tested with simple optimization problem: returns OPTIMAL solution with correct mileage balancing
- **All Validations Pass**: typecheck, lint, build, 1695 tests
- **Remaining Minor Issues** (documented, not blocking):
  - Soft constraint breakdown uses hardcoded ratios (80%/20%) instead of actual solver values
  - `summary.materialBlocks = 0` placeholder, not counted from warnings
  - `test/` directory missing for C++ unit tests

### GAP-2: Report Worker Placeholder Implementation (P2 - HIGH) ✅ RESOLVED (2026-01-20)
- **Location**: `src/workers/processors/report.ts`
- **Status**: ✅ RESOLVED - All data fetching functions implemented with actual database queries and service integrations
- **Implementation**:
  - `fetchPmScheduleData()` - Queries pm_schedule table with joins to trains, fleets, pmTypes, depots, tracks
  - `fetchFleetStatusData()` - Uses FleetService.getAllFleetStatistics() for fleet metrics
  - `fetchDriftReportData()` - Uses DriftMonitorService.runDriftAnalysis() for drift data
  - `fetchTcoProjectionData()` - Uses CostingService.projectTCO() for TCO projections
  - ExcelJS integrated for proper .xlsx generation with formatting and metadata sheets
  - pdfmake integrated for PDF generation with structured layouts, tables, and summaries
- **Dependencies Added**: exceljs@4.4.0, pdfmake@0.3.3, @types/pdfmake@0.2.12
- **All validations pass** (typecheck, lint, build)

### GAP-3: /metrics Endpoint NOT EXPOSED (P2 - HIGH) ✅ RESOLVED (2026-01-20)
- **Location**: `src/api/index.ts`
- **Status**: ✅ RESOLVED - `/metrics` endpoint added returning Prometheus text format
- **Implementation**:
  - Added `GET /metrics` endpoint in `src/api/index.ts:132-137`
  - Returns `getMetrics()` with `text/plain; version=0.0.4` content type
  - 8 unit tests added in `src/api/index.test.ts`
  - All validations pass (typecheck, lint, build)

### GAP-4: Health Check Returns Hardcoded Values (P2 - HIGH) ✅ RESOLVED (2026-01-20)
- **Location**: `src/api/index.ts:117-163`
- **Status**: ✅ RESOLVED - /ready endpoint now performs actual health checks
- **Implementation**:
  - Uses `checkDatabaseHealth()` from `src/lib/db/connection.ts`
  - Uses `checkRedisHealth()` from `src/lib/redis/connection.ts`
  - 5-second timeout for each health check
  - Returns 200 if all services healthy, 503 if any unhealthy
  - 4 new unit tests for healthy/unhealthy scenarios
  - All validations pass (typecheck, lint, build)

### GAP-5: Database Missing Generated Columns (P2 - MEDIUM) ✅ RESOLVED (2026-01-20)
- **Status**: ✅ RESOLVED - All 8 generated columns implemented using Drizzle `.generatedAlwaysAs()`
- **Tables Updated**:
  - `trains.mileage_variance_km` = `current_mileage_km - COALESCE(target_mileage_km, current_mileage_km)`
  - `inventory_positions.quantity_available` = `quantity_on_hand - quantity_reserved`
  - `manhour_projections.variance_hours` = `available_total_hours - required_total_hours`
  - `manhour_projections.utilization_pct` = percentage calculation with null safety
  - `manhour_projections.is_overloaded` = `required_total_hours > available_total_hours`
  - `manhour_projections.is_underutilized` = `required_total_hours < available_total_hours * 0.7`
  - `drift_analysis.mileage_drift_km` = `actual_km - baseline_projected_km`
  - `cost_baselines.variance_pct` = percentage calculation with null safety
- **Benefit**: Computed at database level ensures consistency, simplifies queries, enables indexing

### GAP-6: Database Missing notification_templates Table (P2 - MEDIUM) ✅ RESOLVED (2026-01-20)
- **Location**: `src/lib/db/schema/notifications.ts`
- **Status**: ✅ RESOLVED - `notification_templates` table implemented with full template management
- **Implementation Details**:
  - **Table Structure**: 
    - Template identification: `name`, `type` (enum: EMAIL, SMS, IN_APP, PUSH, WEBHOOK)
    - Template content: `subjectTemplate`, `bodyHtml`, `bodyText` (nullable per type)
    - Template metadata: `description`, `variables` (JSONB array of variable definitions)
    - Scope: `depotId` (optional, for depot-specific template overrides)
    - Status: `isActive`, `version` (for template versioning)
    - Audit: `createdAt`, `updatedAt`, `createdBy`, `updatedBy`
  - **NotificationService Methods**:
    - `getTemplate(templateName, depotId?)` - Hierarchy lookup with fallback to defaults (depot → global)
    - `listTemplates(options?)` - List templates with filtering by type, depot, active status
    - `upsertTemplate(template, userId?)` - Create/update templates with audit trail
    - `deleteTemplate(templateId)` - Logical delete (soft delete via `isActive` flag)
  - **Integration**: 
    - `deliverEmail()` updated to query database templates with fallback to hardcoded defaults
    - Template variables substituted using Handlebars-style syntax (`{{variableName}}`)
    - All exports added to `schema/index.ts` for NotificationTemplate types
- **Impact**: Eliminates hardcoded templates; enables runtime customization per depot without code changes
- **Testing**: All 1695 tests pass
### GAP-7: Database Missing Materialized Views (P2 - MEDIUM) ⏳ SQL READY
- **Status**: SQL definitions COMPLETE, apply via `bun run db:migrate`
- **Required Views**:
  - `v_canvas_strategic` - Yearly PM aggregates per train
  - `v_canvas_tactical` - Weekly PM details
  - `v_manhour_canvas` - Man-hour overlay data
  - `v_drift_canvas` - Drift severity overlay
- **Location**: `drizzle/manual/0004_materialized_views.sql` (340 lines)
- **Deploy**: Migration runner (`src/lib/db/migrator.ts`) auto-applies manual SQL files
- **Note**: Deployment task, not code implementation task

### GAP-8: Database Missing Functions/Triggers (P2 - MEDIUM) ⏳ SQL READY
- **Status**: SQL definitions COMPLETE, apply via `bun run db:migrate`
- **Required**:
  - `sync_train_fitness_from_unavailability()` trigger - Auto-update train fitness
  - `refresh_canvas_views()` function - Concurrent refresh of materialized views
  - `cleanup_expired_scenarios()` function - Scenario expiration
  - `release_expired_reservations()` function - Soft reservation cleanup
  - `update_fleet_mileage_targets()` function - Fleet balancing recalculation
- **Location**: `drizzle/manual/0003_functions.sql` (390 lines)
- **Deploy**: Migration runner auto-applies manual SQL files
- **Note**: Deployment task, not code implementation task

### GAP-9: UI Overlay Panels COMPLETE (P3 - COMPLETE) ✅ VERIFIED 2026-01-21
- **Status**: All overlay panel components fully implemented and verified
- **Completed**:
  - ✅ `ManHourOverlayPanel.tsx` - Weekly depot workload with utilization bars (320px wide, collapsible)
  - ✅ `DriftOverlayPanel.tsx` - Train drift severity with replan recommendations, expandable details
  - ✅ `ScenarioComparisonView.tsx` - Side-by-side scenario vs baseline comparison with delta visualization
  - ✅ `BowWaveIndicator.tsx` - Maintenance clustering risk indicator with peak periods
  - ✅ `src/store/slices/overlay.ts` - State management for all overlay data
  - ✅ App.tsx integration - Panels render when layer toggles active
  - ✅ **Baseline Shadow Layer** (2026-01-20):
    - API endpoint: `GET /canvas/baseline-shadow` - Returns baseline PM positions with status
    - State management: `loadBaselineShadowData()` function in overlay slice
    - `TacticalGanttLayer` renders baseline shadow blocks with status-based coloring (gray ghosted appearance)
    - `InfiniteCanvas` wires up the layer toggle and data loading via overlay state

### GAP-10: Keyboard Shortcuts IMPLEMENTED (P3 - COMPLETE) ✅ VERIFIED 2026-01-21
- **Status**: Fully implemented and verified (2026-01-21)
- **Components Created**:
  - `src/components/canvas/utils/keyboard-handler.ts` - Core keyboard event handling
  - `src/components/canvas/utils/keyboard-handler.test.ts` - 42 unit tests
  - `src/components/KeyboardShortcutsDialog.tsx` - BlueprintJS dialog component
- **InfiniteCanvas Updates**:
  - Added onKeyDown handler with tabIndex for keyboard accessibility
  - Created KeyboardHandlerCallbacks interface for type-safe callbacks
  - Shows "Press ? for keyboard shortcuts" hint in corner
- **Spec 006.9.2 Shortcuts Implemented**:
  - Arrow keys - Pan canvas (Shift for 3x speed)
  - +/- - Zoom in/out
  - 1-5 - Switch zoom levels (Strategic/Tactical-Wide/Tactical/Operational/Detail)
  - t/Home - Go to today
  - s/o - Strategic/Operational view shortcuts
  - Space - Toggle multi-select
  - Enter - Activate selected element
  - Escape - Cancel drag / Close dialog / Clear selection
  - l - Lock/unlock selected PM
  - Delete/Backspace - Delete selected PM (with confirmation)
  - ? - Show keyboard shortcuts help dialog
- **Implementation Constraints**:
  - Uses @belt/tools state (no React hooks per .agentrc.ts)
  - Uses useRef for callbacks (explicitly allowed)
  - Disables shortcuts in input/textarea/contenteditable elements
  - Disables shortcuts when disabled globally (except ? for help)

### ~~GAP-11: Accessibility (WCAG 2.1 AA) INSUFFICIENT~~ ✅ IMPLEMENTED & VERIFIED (2026-01-21)
- **Status**: WCAG 2.1 AA compliance fully implemented and verified for canvas and UI components
- **Implementation**:
  - `src/lib/a11y-announcer.ts` - Screen reader announcements via ARIA live regions
  - `src/lib/focus-management.ts` - Focus navigation, focus ring styles, skip links
  - `src/components/canvas/InfiniteCanvas.tsx` - ARIA attributes, focus handling, reduced motion support
  - `src/state/slices/ui.ts` - Accessibility state (reducedMotion, highContrast, focusVisible)
- **Test Coverage**: 124 new tests (103 pass, 21 skip DOM-dependent)
- **Features Implemented**:
  - Full keyboard navigation (Tab between panels, Enter to activate)
  - ARIA labels on canvas elements
  - Screen reader announcements for PM changes
  - Focus indicators on interactive elements
  - `prefers-reduced-motion` support

### GAP-12: Operations Phase PARTIAL (P3 - MEDIUM)
- **Dockerfiles**: ✅ 5/5 Complete (2026-01-20)
  - ✅ `mms-optimizer/Dockerfile` - C++ OR-Tools optimizer
  - ✅ `docker/Dockerfile.backend` - Bun + Hono API server (<200MB)
  - ✅ `docker/Dockerfile.frontend` - Vite dev + nginx prod (<50MB)
  - ✅ `docker/Dockerfile.worker` - BullMQ background workers (<200MB)
  - ✅ `docker/Dockerfile.file-watcher` - Mileage CSV watcher (<150MB)
- **docker-compose.yml**: ✅ Created (2026-01-20)
  - PostgreSQL 16 + TimescaleDB
  - Redis 7 (queues + caching)
  - All MMS services with health checks
  - Optional tools profile (pgAdmin, Redis Commander)
  - Volume mounts for persistent data
- **.env.example**: ✅ Created with documented variables
- **File Watcher Daemon**: ✅ Created `src/file-watcher/daemon.ts`
- **Kubernetes Manifests**: ❌ Pending (deployments, services, HPA, network policies)
- **CI/CD Pipeline**: ❌ Pending - No `.github/workflows/`
- **Grafana Dashboards**: ❌ Pending (0/6 required per spec 008)
- **Alerting Rules**: ❌ Pending (HighErrorRate, OptimizerSlow, DriftThresholdExceeded, etc.)
- **Network Policies**: ❌ Pending
- **Database Backup Automation**: ❌ Pending

### ~~GAP-13: Integration - Actual Maximo API NOT IMPLEMENTED~~ ✅ RESOLVED (2026-01-20)
- **Location**: `src/services/maximo-client.ts`, `src/services/maximo-sync.ts`
- **Status**: MaximoClient implements actual Maximo OSLC REST API
- **Solution**: Created `MaximoClient` class that:
  - Loads config from `maximo_integration_config` database table
  - Gets password from `MAXIMO_API_PASSWORD` environment variable
  - Falls back to simulation mode if not configured (safe for development)
  - Implements OSLC REST endpoints: POST/GET/PATCH for work orders
  - Integrated with `MaximoSyncService` for push/pull operations

### GAP-14: TODO Comments in Production Code (P2-P3)
- `man-hour-leveling.ts:843-844` - Integrate corrective/overhaul tracking (LOW)
- `costing.ts:322` - Contractor cost calculation placeholder (MEDIUM) ✅ **RESOLVED** (2026-01-20)
  - Implemented `calculateContractorCost()` private method in CostingService
  - Queries APPROVED ADD_CONTRACTOR adjustments from man_hour_adjustments table
  - Contractor premium calculated as: hours * baseRate * (multiplier - 1)
  - CONTRACTOR_COST_MULTIPLIER constant (2.0) aligned with ManHourLevelingService
  - Contractor cost treated as direct cost included in overhead calculation
  - 17 new unit tests covering contractor cost scenarios, edge cases, and audit trails
- `api/index.ts:113` - Add actual health checks (HIGH - same as GAP-4)
- `manhour.ts:127` - Count pending adjustments (LOW)
- `pm-schedule.ts:141` - Get fleet code from relation (LOW)
- `pm-schedule.ts:417-418` - Calculate manhour impact and conflicts (MEDIUM) ✅ **RESOLVED** (2026-01-20)
  - Added `getManHourLevelingService` import and lazy getter in pm-schedule.ts
  - `manhourImpact`: Calculates utilization percentage change between old and new week using `checkLaborCapacity()` from ManHourLevelingService
  - `conflictsCreated`: Since `movePm` already validates and throws on track conflicts, if we reach the response, no conflicts were created (value is correctly 0)

---

## ⚠️ IMMEDIATE BLOCKERS (Fix Before Proceeding)

### BLOCKER-00: Test Infrastructure Verification ✅ VERIFIED (2026-01-20)

**Verification Request**: Confirm Ralph Wiggum methodology requires tests before delivery.

**Current State**:
- ✅ **Unit Tests**: 700+ tests across 22 test files covering:
  - Domain Services (fleet, pm-scheduler, baseline, drift-monitor, costing, etc.)
  - API Routes (canvas, costing, material, scenario)
  - Library Utilities (date, resilience, transaction, errors)
  - Optimizer (fallback algorithm)
- ✅ **Code Quality Hooks** (`.claude/hooks/`):
  - `block-forbidden-packages.sh` - Blocks zustand, redux, zod, xstate, @mui, antd, tailwind, next.js
  - `block-forbidden-imports.sh` - Blocks forbidden imports in src/
  - `session-start.sh` - Audits constraints at session start
- ✅ **Agent Config** (`.agentrc.ts`):
  - Blocks React hooks (useState, useEffect, etc.) - forces @belt/tools state machines
  - Blocks Co-Authored-By commit lines
- ✅ **Documented Validation Workflow** (AGENTS.md):
  1. `bun test <path>` - Unit tests for changed files
  2. `bun run typecheck` - TypeScript strict mode
  3. `bun run lint` - ESLint code quality
  4. `bun run build` - Full build verification
  5. Commit only after ALL validations pass

**Dependencies for Full Automation**:
- ⏳ **GitHub Actions CI/CD** - Phase 8.7 (P3) - Will automate validation pipeline
- ⏳ **Playwright E2E Tests** - Requires Phase 7 UI implementation (currently ~1% complete)
- ⏳ **Pre-commit Hooks** - Optional enhancement (manual workflow currently documented)

**Conclusion**: Test infrastructure is comprehensive and enforced. Full CI/CD automation is planned in Phase 8.7. Playwright tests will be added when frontend is implemented (Phase 7).

### BLOCKER-1: PMSchedulerService NOT Exported (CRITICAL) ✅ RESOLVED
- **Issue**: `PMSchedulerService` is fully implemented (2,174 lines, 42 passing tests) but NOT exported in `src/services/index.ts`
- **Impact**: Cannot be imported by API routes, workers, or other services
- **Fix**: Add export to `src/services/index.ts`
- **Resolution**: Export added to `src/services/index.ts` on 2026-01-19

### BLOCKER-2: @belt/tools Migration (CRITICAL) ✅ RESOLVED
- **Issue**: Project uses `zod` for validation but README mandates `@belt/tools`
- **Issue**: `zustand` is in package.json but BLOCKED by `.claude/hooks/block-forbidden-packages.sh`
- **Issue**: React hooks (useState, useEffect) BLOCKED by `.agentrc.ts`
- **Impact**: Cannot build compliant UI; hooks will be rejected by automation
- **Fix**: Complete Phase 1.3 migration before any UI work
- **Resolution**: Migrated all Zod schemas to @belt/tools createSchema/createType, removed zod and zustand from package.json on 2026-01-20. All 384 tests pass.

### BLOCKER-3: Missing Lib Utility Tests (HIGH) ✅ RESOLVED
- **Issue**: Critical utilities have no test coverage:
  - `src/lib/utils/date.ts` - Compliance window calculations (CRITICAL for PM scheduling) ✅ Tests complete (90 tests)
  - `src/lib/utils/resilience.ts` - Circuit breaker logic (CRITICAL for Maximo integration) ✅ Tests complete (57 tests)
  - `src/lib/utils/transaction.ts` - Transaction rollback behavior ✅ Tests complete (51 tests)
  - `src/lib/errors/index.ts` - Error serialization ✅ Tests complete (110 tests)
- **Impact**: Bugs in these utilities cascade to all domain services
- **Resolution**: All critical lib utilities now have comprehensive test coverage (308 tests total)

---

## Current State Analysis

**Codebase Status**: API & Services Complete, UI Overlays & Operations Pending

### ✅ COMPLETE - Phase 1.1: Database Schema (100%)
- 40+ tables across 14 schema files (Drizzle ORM)
- 27 PostgreSQL enums defined (16 required + 11 additional)
- TimescaleDB hypertable configuration (7-day chunks, 30-day compression, 13-year retention)
- SQL definitions for materialized views exist in `drizzle/manual/0004_materialized_views.sql`
- SQL definitions for functions/triggers exist in `drizzle/manual/0003_functions.sql`

### 1.3 @belt/tools Migration (P1 - Critical)

> **CRITICAL**: Project uses Zod for validation but README mandates @belt/tools.
> zustand is in package.json but BLOCKED by .claude/hooks/block-forbidden-packages.sh
> React hooks (useState, useEffect, etc.) are BLOCKED by .agentrc.ts - use machine.useMachine()

#### Schema Migration (Zod → @belt/tools) ✅ COMPLETE (2026-01-20)

#### State Machine Setup (for Phase 7 UI) ✅ COMPLETE (Phase 7.4)
  - Also implemented: scenarioSlice, uiSlice (src/store/slices/scenario.ts, src/store/slices/ui.ts)

#### Reference: @belt/tools Patterns
```typescript
// Schema validation (replaces Zod)
import { createSchema, createType, InferType } from '@belt/tools';

const TrainModel = createSchema({
  id: 'string',
  trainNumber: 'string',
  mileageKm: 'number',
  fitnessStatus: { enum: ['FIT', 'RESTRICTED', 'GROUNDED'] },
  tags: 'string[]',
  metadata: 'object?',
});
type Train = InferType<typeof TrainModel>;
const train = TrainModel.parse(data);

// State machine (replaces React hooks + zustand)
import { createSlice, createMachine } from '@belt/tools';

const fleetSlice = createSlice({
  id: 'fleet',
  initial: { trains: [], selected: null },
  cache: { policy: 'session' },
  reducers: {
    setTrains: (state, trains) => { state.trains = trains; },
    selectTrain: (state, id) => { state.selected = id; },
  },
});

const machine = createMachine({
  id: 'mms',
  slices: { fleet: fleetSlice },
  trackMetadata: false, // Critical for 60fps
});

// In React component (NO useState/useEffect!)
const { state, actions } = machine.useMachine('fleet');
```

**RESOLVED Test Infrastructure Issue (2026-01-20)**:
- Issue: Export service tests (`src/services/export.test.ts`) had a mock.module() call for the tracing module that caused test isolation issues
- Root cause: The mock didn't properly simulate withSpan behavior - it called fn() synchronously instead of awaiting, didn't pass a span object, and was missing addSpanEvent and other tracing utilities
- Impact: The improper mock "leaked" and affected resilience tests when running the full test suite
- Fix: Updated the mock in src/services/export.test.ts to properly handle async functions, pass a mock span object with setAttribute/setStatus/recordException/end methods, and include addSpanEvent mock
- Status: All 1588 tests now pass consistently

## Critical Dependencies

| Corrective maintenance status | Maximo webhook | PM scheduling validity | Manual status UI |

| Route utilization profiles | Operations input | Mileage projections | Fleet-wide average (warning flag) |

---

## Notes

1. **Non-negotiable dependencies**: Corrective maintenance visibility and service demand are prerequisites for valid schedules
2. **Dual-solver architecture**: Strategic (GA) and tactical (OR-Tools CP-SAT) must share constraint envelope
3. **Explainability**: Every optimizer decision must include factor-based explanation traces
4. **Baseline/drift**: Long-term plans stabilize via published baselines; drift thresholds prevent jitter
5. **Material constraints**: PM scheduling without parts availability is operationally useless
6. **Cold-start risk**: Historical PM data will be incomplete - explicit confidence scoring required
7. **Audit compliance**: 12-year government contract mandates immutable audit trail with full attribution
8. **Maximo Integration API (2026-01-20)**: Integration API routes registered at /api/v1/integration/*. Webhook validates event types: wo_status_changed, wo_created, wo_updated, wo_cancelled. Webhook updates shadow records and PM schedule status automatically. Status-based locking implemented (INPRG, COMP, CLOSE, CAN lock PMs from editing in MMS).

## 🔴 CRITICAL GAPS (2026-01-20 Multi-Agent Deep Scan Findings)

### GAP-1: C++ Optimizer Binary NOT COMPILED (P1 - CRITICAL) ✅ RESOLVED (2026-01-20)
- **Location**: `mms-optimizer/`
- **Status**: ✅ RESOLVED - Binary compiled and tested
- **Resolution**:
  - Installed dependencies via Homebrew: `cmake`, `or-tools`, `nlohmann-json`, `fmt`
  - Fixed unused parameter warnings in `solver.cpp` (lines 574, 655)
  - Fixed lambda capture issue in `main.cpp` (line 142 - static variable `g_requestId`)
  - Compiled binary at `mms-optimizer/build/mms-optimizer` (418KB)
  - Updated TypeScript wrapper default path to `./mms-optimizer/build/mms-optimizer`
  - Added `mms-optimizer/build` to eslint ignore patterns
  - Binary tested with simple optimization problem: returns OPTIMAL solution with correct mileage balancing
- **All Validations Pass**: typecheck, lint, build, 1695 tests
- **Remaining Minor Issues** (documented, not blocking):
  - Soft constraint breakdown uses hardcoded ratios (80%/20%) instead of actual solver values
  - `summary.materialBlocks = 0` placeholder, not counted from warnings

### GAP-2: Report Worker Placeholder Implementation (P2 - HIGH) ✅ RESOLVED (2026-01-20)
- **Location**: `src/workers/processors/report.ts`
- **Status**: ✅ RESOLVED - All data fetching functions implemented with actual database queries and service integrations
- **Implementation**:
  - `fetchPmScheduleData()` - Queries pm_schedule table with joins to trains, fleets, pmTypes, depots, tracks
  - `fetchFleetStatusData()` - Uses FleetService.getAllFleetStatistics() for fleet metrics
  - `fetchDriftReportData()` - Uses DriftMonitorService.runDriftAnalysis() for drift data
  - `fetchTcoProjectionData()` - Uses CostingService.projectTCO() for TCO projections
  - ExcelJS integrated for proper .xlsx generation with formatting and metadata sheets
  - pdfmake integrated for PDF generation with structured layouts, tables, and summaries
- **Dependencies Added**: exceljs@4.4.0, pdfmake@0.3.3, @types/pdfmake@0.2.12
- **All validations pass** (typecheck, lint, build)

### GAP-3: /metrics Endpoint NOT EXPOSED (P2 - HIGH) ✅ RESOLVED (2026-01-20)

- **Status**: ✅ RESOLVED - `/metrics` endpoint added returning Prometheus text format

  - Returns `getMetrics()` with `text/plain; version=0.0.4` content type
  - 8 unit tests added in `src/api/index.test.ts`

### GAP-4: Health Check Returns Hardcoded Values (P2 - HIGH) ✅ RESOLVED (2026-01-20)
- **Location**: `src/api/index.ts:117-163`
- **Status**: ✅ RESOLVED - /ready endpoint now performs actual health checks
- **Implementation**:
  - Uses `checkDatabaseHealth()` from `src/lib/db/connection.ts`
  - Uses `checkRedisHealth()` from `src/lib/redis/connection.ts`
  - 5-second timeout for each health check
  - Returns 200 if all services healthy, 503 if any unhealthy
  - 4 new unit tests for healthy/unhealthy scenarios
  - All validations pass (typecheck, lint, build)

### GAP-5: Database Missing Generated Columns (P2 - MEDIUM) ✅ RESOLVED (2026-01-20)
- **Status**: ✅ RESOLVED - All 8 generated columns implemented using Drizzle `.generatedAlwaysAs()`
- **Tables Updated**:
  - `trains.mileage_variance_km` = `current_mileage_km - COALESCE(target_mileage_km, current_mileage_km)`
  - `inventory_positions.quantity_available` = `quantity_on_hand - quantity_reserved`
  - `manhour_projections.variance_hours` = `available_total_hours - required_total_hours`
  - `manhour_projections.utilization_pct` = percentage calculation with null safety
  - `manhour_projections.is_overloaded` = `required_total_hours > available_total_hours`
  - `manhour_projections.is_underutilized` = `required_total_hours < available_total_hours * 0.7`
  - `drift_analysis.mileage_drift_km` = `actual_km - baseline_projected_km`
  - `cost_baselines.variance_pct` = percentage calculation with null safety
- **Benefit**: Computed at database level ensures consistency, simplifies queries, enables indexing

### GAP-6: Database Missing notification_templates Table (P2 - MEDIUM) ✅ RESOLVED (2026-01-20)
- **Location**: `src/lib/db/schema/notifications.ts`
- **Status**: ✅ RESOLVED - `notification_templates` table implemented with full template management
- **Implementation Details**:
  - **Table Structure**: 
    - Template identification: `name`, `type` (enum: EMAIL, SMS, IN_APP, PUSH, WEBHOOK)
    - Template content: `subjectTemplate`, `bodyHtml`, `bodyText` (nullable per type)
    - Template metadata: `description`, `variables` (JSONB array of variable definitions)
    - Scope: `depotId` (optional, for depot-specific template overrides)
    - Status: `isActive`, `version` (for template versioning)
    - Audit: `createdAt`, `updatedAt`, `createdBy`, `updatedBy`
  - **NotificationService Methods**:
    - `getTemplate(templateName, depotId?)` - Hierarchy lookup with fallback to defaults (depot → global)
    - `listTemplates(options?)` - List templates with filtering by type, depot, active status
    - `upsertTemplate(template, userId?)` - Create/update templates with audit trail
    - `deleteTemplate(templateId)` - Logical delete (soft delete via `isActive` flag)
  - **Integration**: 
    - `deliverEmail()` updated to query database templates with fallback to hardcoded defaults
    - Template variables substituted using Handlebars-style syntax (`{{variableName}}`)
    - All exports added to `schema/index.ts` for NotificationTemplate types
- **Impact**: Eliminates hardcoded templates; enables runtime customization per depot without code changes

  - Also implemented: scenarioSlice, uiSlice (src/store/slices/scenario.ts, src/store/slices/ui.ts)

#### Reference: @belt/tools Patterns
```typescript
// Schema validation (replaces Zod)
import { createSchema, createType, InferType } from '@belt/tools';

const TrainModel = createSchema({
  id: 'string',
  trainNumber: 'string',
  mileageKm: 'number',
  fitnessStatus: { enum: ['FIT', 'RESTRICTED', 'GROUNDED'] },
  tags: 'string[]',
  metadata: 'object?',
});
type Train = InferType<typeof TrainModel>;
const train = TrainModel.parse(data);

// State machine (replaces React hooks + zustand)
import { createSlice, createMachine } from '@belt/tools';

const fleetSlice = createSlice({
  id: 'fleet',
  initial: { trains: [], selected: null },
  cache: { policy: 'session' },
  reducers: {
    setTrains: (state, trains) => { state.trains = trains; },
    selectTrain: (state, id) => { state.selected = id; },
  },
});

const machine = createMachine({
  id: 'mms',
  slices: { fleet: fleetSlice },
  trackMetadata: false, // Critical for 60fps
});

// In React component (NO useState/useEffect!)
const { state, actions } = machine.useMachine('fleet');
```

**RESOLVED Test Infrastructure Issue (2026-01-20)**:
- Issue: Export service tests (`src/services/export.test.ts`) had a mock.module() call for the tracing module that caused test isolation issues
- Root cause: The mock didn't properly simulate withSpan behavior - it called fn() synchronously instead of awaiting, didn't pass a span object, and was missing addSpanEvent and other tracing utilities
- Impact: The improper mock "leaked" and affected resilience tests when running the full test suite
- Fix: Updated the mock in src/services/export.test.ts to properly handle async functions, pass a mock span object with setAttribute/setStatus/recordException/end methods, and include addSpanEvent mock
- Status: All 1588 tests now pass consistently

## Critical Dependencies

| Dependency | Source | Required For | Fallback |
|-----------|--------|--------------|----------|
| Corrective maintenance status | Maximo webhook | PM scheduling validity | Manual status UI |

| Route utilization profiles | Operations input | Mileage projections | Fleet-wide average (warning flag) |

## Notes

1. **Non-negotiable dependencies**: Corrective maintenance visibility and service demand are prerequisites for valid schedules
2. **Dual-solver architecture**: Strategic (GA) and tactical (OR-Tools CP-SAT) must share constraint envelope
3. **Explainability**: Every optimizer decision must include factor-based explanation traces
4. **Baseline/drift**: Long-term plans stabilize via published baselines; drift thresholds prevent jitter
5. **Material constraints**: PM scheduling without parts availability is operationally useless
6. **Cold-start risk**: Historical PM data will be incomplete - explicit confidence scoring required
7. **Audit compliance**: 12-year government contract mandates immutable audit trail with full attribution
8. **Maximo Integration API (2026-01-20)**: Integration API routes registered at /api/v1/integration/*. Webhook validates event types: wo_status_changed, wo_created, wo_updated, wo_cancelled. Webhook updates shadow records and PM schedule status automatically. Status-based locking implemented (INPRG, COMP, CLOSE, CAN lock PMs from editing in MMS).

**RESOLVED Test Infrastructure Issue (2026-01-20)**:
- Issue: Export service tests (`src/services/export.test.ts`) had a mock.module() call for the tracing module that caused test isolation issues
- Root cause: The mock didn't properly simulate withSpan behavior - it called fn() synchronously instead of awaiting, didn't pass a span object, and was missing addSpanEvent and other tracing utilities
- Impact: The improper mock "leaked" and affected resilience tests when running the full test suite
- Fix: Updated the mock in src/services/export.test.ts to properly handle async functions, pass a mock span object with setAttribute/setStatus/recordException/end methods, and include addSpanEvent mock
- Status: All 1588 tests now pass consistently
